title: Ceraia niveobarbata
taxonomy: Ceraia niveobarbata is a species of moth in the family Sphingidae. The classification of Ceraia niveobarbata can be broken down as follows: kingdom Animalia, phylum Arthropoda, class Insecta, order Lepidoptera, and family Sphingidae. This classification reflects the moth's position within the insect family. As a member of the family Sphingidae, Ceraia niveobarbata is closely related to other hawk moths. Its classification is used in the identification and study of this species.

intro: Ceraia niveobarbata, also known as White-bearded Orchid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Traditionally, Ceraia niveobarbata has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ceraia niveobarbata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ceraia niveobarbata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate respiratory problems, and it is also used in some Asian cultures to make a medicinal soup.
